meta.title: Fire detection and fire alarm systems for buildings — Code of practice for design, installation, commissioning and maintenance
widely_used_in: Gulf countries (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait)

risk_categories.description: BS 5839 uses Category System (L, P, M) to define the level of protection required

L1.description: Maximum life protection — detectors throughout ALL areas of the building (except voids < 800mm)
L1.coverage: All rooms, circulation areas, stairwells, lift shafts, boiler rooms, storage rooms, toilets
L1.typical_use: Hospitals, care homes, hotels, large public buildings, high-risk residential

L2.description: Life protection in specific areas — detectors in all rooms defined in Table 1 of BS 5839-1
L2.coverage: All escape routes, all rooms opening onto escape routes, rooms with high fire risk
L2.typical_use: Offices, schools, shops, restaurants, medium-sized commercial

L3.description: Life protection for escape routes — detectors protecting escape routes and rooms opening directly onto them
L3.coverage: All corridors, lobbies, stairwells, lift lobbies, hallways, any room opening directly onto escape route
L3.typical_use: Small offices, small shops, light industrial, commercial premises

L4.description: Minimum life protection — detectors on escape routes ONLY
L4.coverage: Corridors, stairwells, hallways leading to final exits only
L4.typical_use: Small premises, HMO (Houses in Multiple Occupation), very small commercial

L5.description: Custom life protection — designer selects areas based on specific fire risk assessment
L5.coverage: As determined by fire risk assessment — may include any combination of areas
L5.typical_use: Unique buildings, specific risk areas, heritage buildings
L5.notes: Requires documented fire risk assessment justification

P1.description: Maximum property protection — detectors throughout ALL areas
P1.coverage: All rooms, voids, risers, ceiling spaces, floor/ceiling voids > 800mm
P1.typical_use: Data centers, museums, archives, high-value storage, insurance requirement
P1.notes: May include aspirating detection in sensitive areas

P2.description: Property protection in defined areas — detectors in specific rooms identified by risk assessment
P2.coverage: High-value rooms, areas with high fire load, critical infrastructure areas
P2.typical_use: Server rooms, switch rooms, document storage, stock rooms

M.title: Manual System Only
M.description: Manual call points only — no automatic detection
M.coverage: Call points at all exits and strategic locations
M.typical_use: Very small premises, low-risk open-plan areas, temporary buildings
M.limitation: Only suitable where fire will be detected by occupants before escape routes are compromised

combined_systems.description: Many installations use combined categories, e.g. L3 + P2 for mixed life/property protection

point_type_smoke.notes: Spacing can be increased by 50% (to 11.25m) in corridors less than 2m wide

fixed_temperature.notes: Less sensitive — not preferred for life safety alone

combined_ROR_fixed.notes: Most commonly used heat detector type

multi_criteria_detector.description: Combines smoke, heat, and sometimes CO sensing in one device
multi_criteria_detector.notes: Preferred for areas prone to false alarms

beam_detector.max_spacing_between_beams: 12.5m from any point to nearest beam
beam_detector.notes: Can also be reflective type (transmitter + reflector)

aspirating_smoke_detector.description: Pipe-based system drawing air samples to a central detector unit
aspirating_smoke_detector.sampling_points: Up to 100 per pipe run (manufacturer dependent)
aspirating_smoke_detector.pipe_length_max: Up to 200m total pipe length (varies by manufacturer)
aspirating_smoke_detector.typical_use: Clean rooms, data centers, cold stores, atria, high-ceiling areas

corridor_spacing.rule: Spacing can be increased by 50% in corridors less than 2m wide
corridor_spacing.notes: Only applies when corridor width is ≤ 2m

ceiling_height_limits.description: BS 5839 defines maximum ceiling heights for reliable detection

limits.max_height_with_justification: 12.5m (EN54-7 Class A1, A2, or B only)

sloped_ceilings.slope_30_to_60_degrees: Detectors within 600mm of ridge AND within 600mm of eaves
sloped_ceilings.slope_over_60_degrees: Treat as vertical surface — detectors on ceiling AND upper wall

beamed_ceilings.general: Detectors generally not required in beam pockets if beam depth < 10% of ceiling height
beamed_ceilings.beam_depth_10_to_20_percent: May need additional detectors — engineering judgment required

vents_and_diffusers.rule: Avoid placing detectors directly in the path of HVAC supply air

location_requirements.at_all_exits: On every floor at every exit
location_requirements.height: 1.2m to 1.4m from finished floor level (operating element)

travel_distance.max_to_nearest_call_point: 30m measured along the route of travel (45m in exceptional circumstances with justification)
travel_distance.notes: Measures along the normal path of travel, not direct distance

mounting.to_operating_element: Measured to the break-glass element or operating lever

additional_locations: At each change of floor level in stairwells
At each change of direction in corridors > 45m
Near specific hazards identified by risk assessment
At entrance to dead-end corridors exceeding 9m length
At fire doors that form part of escape route

sounders.minimum_sound_level: 65 dBA OR 5 dBA above ambient noise level, whichever is greater
sounders.minimum_at_bedhead: 75 dBA in sleeping areas (hospitals, hotels, care homes)
sounders.pulse_pattern: Distinct pulse (500-1000Hz tones, not steady) — per EN 54-3

spacing.method: Calculate based on sounder output rating and ambient noise
spacing.rule_of_thumb: Typically 7.5m to 15m between sounders depending on output
spacing.overlap: Coverage circles must overlap to ensure minimum level everywhere
spacing.notes: Must verify with acoustic calculation or field measurement

visual_alarm_devices.description: Required where ambient noise exceeds 90 dBA, or for hearing-impaired occupants
visual_alarm_devices.type: Xenon flash (red or white per local requirements)
visual_alarm_devices.notes: BS 5839 does not specify candela values like NFPA 72 — follows EN 54-23

voice_alarm.description: Voice alarm systems (VAS) for phased evacuation
voice_alarm.typical_use: High-rise, large retail, transport terminals, entertainment venues
voice_alarm.notes: BS 5839-8 is the dedicated standard for voice alarm systems

general_rules.description: BS 5839 requires zones to help identify the location of a fire
general_rules.max_floor_per_zone: One zone should not cover more than one floor (except where floor area < 300m²)
general_rules.max_search_distance: 60m — the distance a firefighter must travel to identify the exact location of a fire within a zone
general_rules.max_detectors_per_conventional_zone: 20 detectors per zone (recommended for conventional systems)
general_rules.addressable_systems: Zones are virtual (software-defined) but same area rules apply

zone_labeling.format: Zone 01, Zone 02, etc. — with clear floor plan reference
zone_labeling.display: Each zone must be clearly identified on the control panel and annunciator
zone_labeling.zone_plan: Zone chart must be displayed adjacent to the control panel

special_zones.fire_doors: Should be in separate zone from adjacent areas
special_zones.lift_shafts: Each shaft should be a separate zone
special_zones.stairwells: Should be separate from floor areas

fire_resistant.description: Cables that maintain circuit integrity under fire conditions (typically 30 min, 60 min, or 120 min resistance)
fire_resistant.designation: FP200, FP400, FP Plus, MINERAL insulated (MICC), etc.
required_for: All circuits in Category L1/L2 systems

permitted_for: Detector circuits in L3/L4 systems (where not required for evacuation)
Monitoring circuits where life safety is not dependent
standard_cable.notes: Must still be suitable for the environment and comply with BS 7671 (Wiring Regulations)

cable_routing.separation: Fire alarm cables should be separated from other services by minimum 50mm or use segregated trunking
cable_routing.avoid: Do not route near high-voltage cables, fluorescent lighting, or sources of EMI

circuit_designations.description: BS 5839 uses similar concepts to NFPA for circuit integrity

types.fire_resistant_to_standard: Fire-resistant cable maintaining integrity per BS EN 50200 PH30/PH60/PH120
types.enhanced_fire_resistant: For highest criticality — longer fire resistance periods

panel_location.requirement: At an entrance used by fire service or in a position agreed with fire service
panel_location.height: Centre of panel at 1.4m to 1.6m from floor
panel_location.access: Dedicated room or area with clear access (minimum 500mm clearance in front)
panel_location.lighting: Adequate lighting for reading panel display

display_requirements.minimum_display: LED indicators for Alarm, Fault, Disable (Isolate) per zone
display_requirements.text_display: Alpha-numeric display showing event details (standard in modern panels)

power_supplies.secondary: Standby batteries — minimum 24 hours standby in normal condition
power_supplies.secondary_alarm: Minimum 30 minutes alarm duration after standby period
power_supplies.voice_alarm_secondary: Minimum 24 hours standby + 60 minutes voice alarm
power_supplies.battery_monitoring: Charger fault and battery disconnect must be indicated as fault condition

door_release.description: Fire doors held open by electromagnetic holders must be released on fire alarm
door_release.action: All electromagnetic holders released to allow fire doors to close
door_release.power: Must fail-safe — doors close on power failure

ventilation.description: HVAC system shutdown or smoke control activation
ventilation.action: Close fire dampers, shutdown air handling units, activate smoke clearance

lift_control.description: Recall lifts to designated level on fire alarm
lift_control.action: All lifts recalled to designated level (typically ground floor)

fire_suppression.description: Interfacing with sprinkler, gas suppression, or other suppression systems
fire_suppression.monitoring: Water flow, valve status, suppression system activation

periodic_testing.weekly: Test one manual call point (rotate to cover all within 13 weeks), check panel for faults
periodic_testing.quarterly: Test automatic detectors in high-risk areas (as per fire risk assessment)
periodic_testing.annual: Full system test — all detectors, call points, sounders tested (100%)
periodic_testing.service_visit: At least twice per year for Category L systems (quarterly recommended)

special_inspection.description: Additional inspections may be required after:
events: After any fault that affected system operation

categories.unwanted_alarm: Alarm caused by non-fire stimulus (cooking, steam, dust) — device operates as designed
categories.false_alarm: Alarm caused by equipment malfunction or damage
categories.equipment_false_alarm: Due to poor design, installation, or maintenance

mitigation_strategies: Correct detector type selection (avoid smoke detectors in kitchens/bathrooms)
Proper siting away from air vents and heat sources
Staff training on causes and prevention

alarm_verification.description: Where permitted, a second detection event can be required before full alarm
requirements: Only permitted in P-category (property protection) systems
NOT permitted in L-category (life protection) systems
Must automatically escalate to full alarm if second event occurs
